text: The University of Pennsylvania Medical Center: Center for Sleep and Respiratory Neurobiology 1 | P a g e Digital Morphometrics Laser Method – Procedure SOP Figure 1.1 – Camera and Laser Device Mounted on Monopod Digital Morphometrics Laser Method – Procedure SOP 1. Craniofacial Photographic Procedure 1.1 Equipment and Setup (for more detailed descriptions see ―Digital Morphometrics – Equipment and Setup SOP‖) Consumer digital camera (e.g. Powershot SX120 IS, Canon Inc., Japan) with key features: manual focus at 40cm and a automatic exposure setting Laser device will be provided Monopod (e.g. Manfrotto 682B (3231) Professional Monopod with Detachable Legs) Connect the camera, laser device, and monopod with provided screws (Fig. 1.1) Backless Bench Marker or pencil suitable for skin with appropriate removal technique 1.2 Subject Preparation Subjects with long hair should have it tied back such that both ears and the posterior neck exposed Accessories such as glasses and necklaces should be removed Reasonable efforts should be made to expose to the level of the sterna notch (subject’s comfort level should be taken into consideration) Surface markings identified by palpation and marked using a pen (Fig. 1.2B and C, Arrows) 1. Condylion (co) – Palpate the area of skin just anterior to the ear canal and feel the lateral pole of the condyle o TIP: Ask the subject to open and close his/her mouth and shift his/her jaw side to side for accurate identification (Fig. 1.2B). 2. Gonion Profile (go) – Identify and mark the most posterior and lateral point on the external angle of the mandible (Fig. 1.2B). 3. Gonion Neck Extended (goR/goL) –With the subjects neck maximally extended palpate and mark the most posterior and inferior angle of the mandible. o TIP: When palpating, be careful not to shift the skin away from the gonion landmark (Fig. 1.2C) 1.3 Subject Positioning Camera should be positioned 40cm from the subject Use a plain background wall Instructions given to the subject prior to obtaining the frontal and profile photographs o Seated position with his/her hips and back (to the best of his/her ability) against the wall o Natural head position (achieved by asking the subject to ―look straight ahead as if looking into a mirror‖) o Ask the subject to maintain a neutral, resting facial expression; keep the mouth closed with lips lighting touching; and breathe quietly Instructions given to the subject prior to obtaining the neck extended photograph o Seated position on the end of the bench (to allow full neck extension), while maintaining proper posture o Neck extended position (achieve by asking to ―while sitting up straight, lean your neck back to look up at the ceiling‖; if necessary assist by guiding the subject’s neck posteriorly while ensuring correct posture o With the neck extended ensure visualization of nostrils when taking this photograph 2 | P a g e Digital Morphometrics Laser Method – Procedure SOP (A) Frontal Photograph (Fig. 1.2A) The subject’s face is lined up in the center of the camera display Ensure both ears are equally seen on the frontal view Point lasers approximately 2cm from lower lip on a flat plane of the chin (B) Profile Photograph (Fig. 1.2B) Ensure the full profile is visible Point lasers approximately 2cm from ear canal on a flat plane of the cheek, without obstructing the condylion (C) Neck Extended Photograph (Fig. 1.2C) Ensure neck is bent back such that both ears are equally visible Point at the subject’s neck, centered between the left and right gonion 1.4 Remove all markings Figure 1.2 – Craniofacial photographs: (A) Frontal photograph with lasers pointed approximately 2cm from lower lip on a flat plane of the chin. (B) Profile photograph with arrows pointing to condylion (co) and gonion (go) landmarks (as identified through palpation and marked with liquid eyeliner) and lasers pointing approximately 2cm from ear canal on a flat plane of the cheek. (C) Neck extended photograph with arrows pointing to the right and left gonion (goR/goL) landmarks (as identified through palpation and marked with liquid eyeliner) and the lasers centered between the left and right gonion 3 | P a g e Digital Morphometrics Laser Method – Procedure SOP Figure 2.1 – Camera-Laser system with clip light, mounted on monopod 2. Intra-Oral Photographic Procedure 2.1 Equipment and Setup (for detailed descriptions see Equipment and Setup SOP) Consumer digital camera (e.g. Powershot SX120 IS, Canon Inc., Japan) with key features: manual focus at 40cm and automatic exposure setting Laser device will be provided Monopod (e.g. Manfrotto 682B (3231)Professional Monopod with Detachable Legs) Clip light with easily adjustable mount (e.g. Coast Clip Light, Model #753) Connect the camera and laser device with clip light to the monopod with provided screws (Fig. 2.1 ) Backless Bench Two tongue depressors Tongue depressor holder to fit 2 stacked tongue depressors 2.2 Subject Positioning Camera system should be positioned approximately 40cm from the subject Seat the subject on the backless bench with his/her hips and back (to the best of his/her ability) against the wall, while guiding the patient`s head to obtain a natural head position Instructions given to the subject prior to obtaining any intra-oral photographs o ―Show your teeth and smile.‖ This provides the best visualization of the oral cavity without obstruction by the lips o Instruct the subject to open his/her mouth until he reaches the full range of vertical motion Note: do not have the subject over extend, for this can distort intraoral structures Ensure that the subject does not tilt his/her head back or forward when opening his/her mouth Aim the clip light to illuminate the airway. This requires adjusting its directionality to maximize visualization. Ensure relaxed tongue. If the tongue is not relaxed try these troubleshooting techniques: o Instruct the patient to breathe rhythmically through his/her mouth. o Use a mirror and example picture to help the subject understand what is being asked. o Place a tongue depressor gently along the subjects tongue o If the subject’s tongue appears to be uncomfortably constrained by the mandible, instruct the subject to extend his/her tongue a tiny bit to give the tongue space to relax (A) Open Mouth, No Phonation (Fig. 2.2) Point the lasers at the junction of the hard and soft palate. If not possible, point the lasers directly at the airway or the base of the tongue. (B) Open Mouth, With Phonation (Figure 2.3) Point the lasers at the junction of the hard and soft palate. If not possible, point the lasers directly at the airway or the base of the tongue. Instruct the subject to say ―ahhh‖ for the duration of the photograph 4 | P a g e Digital Morphometrics Laser Method – Procedure SOP Figure 2.2 –Open Mouth, No Phonation Figure 2.3 – Open Mouth, With Phonation (C) Open Mouth, Tongue Out (Fig. 2.4) Instruct the subject to extend his/her tongue o The subject should have his/her tongue come out to its fullest extent, in a relaxed fashion, similar to that of a panting dog Point the lasers at the junction of the hard and soft palate. If not possible, point the lasers directly at the airway or the base of the tongue (D) Open Mouth, Tongue Out, Profile (Fig. 2.5) Instruct the subject to extend his/her tongue o The subject should have his/her tongue come out to its fullest extent, in a relaxed fashion, similar to that of a panting dog Position camera to the side of the subject and visualize the profile. Point the lasers approx 2cm from ear canal on flattest plane not obstructing condylion landmark Figure 2.5— Open Mouth, Tongue Out, Profile Figure 2.4 – Open Mouth, Tongue out 5 | P a g e Digital Morphometrics Laser Method – Procedure SOP (E) Open Mouth, With Tongue Depressor, No Phonation (Fig. 2.6) Depress the tongue blade posteriorly with enough force so that the airway and uvula can be visualized (F) Open Mouth, With Tongue Depressor, with Phonation (Fig. 2.7) Depress the tongue blade posteriorly with enough force so that the airway and uvula can be visualized Instruct the subject to say ―ahhh‖ for the duration of the photograph Figure 2.6 – Open Mouth, With Tongue Depressor, No Phonation Figure 2.7 – Open Mouth, With Tongue Depressor, With Phonation
